```{r ITS_liberation_figure}
cat("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n\n")


# Fit Kaplan-Meier model (event = successful liberation)
liberation_fit <- survfit(
  Surv(timetoliberation, liberation_success) ~ Candida_Dominance_3cat,
  data = facts_its_rel_analysis
)

# Cox model
cox_liberation <- coxph(
  Surv(timetoliberation, liberation_success) ~ Candida_Dominance_3cat,
  data = facts_its_rel_analysis
)

# Create KM plot (cumulative incidence of liberation)
fig_liberation <- ggsurvplot(
  liberation_fit,
  data = facts_its_rel_analysis,
  pval = TRUE,
  pval.method = TRUE,
  pval.coord = c(30, 0.2),
  risk.table = TRUE,
  tables.height = 0.25,
  fun = "event",  # Plot cumulative incidence of event (liberation)
  censor.shape = "|",
  censor = TRUE,
  title = "Time to Successful Ventilator Liberation by Candida Dominance",
  xlab = "Days from Intubation",
  ylab = "Cumulative Liberation Rate",
  legend.labs = c("C. albicans", "Other Candida", "No Candida Dominance"),
  palette = c("brown3", "pink2", "gray7"),
  tables.theme = clean_theme(),
  font.title = c(14, "bold", "black"),
  font.legend = c(12, "bold", "black"),
  font.x = c(11, "bold", "black"),
  font.y = c(11, "bold", "black"), xlim = c(0, 90),  # ← LIMIT X-AXIS TO 90 DAYS
                       break.time.by = 15,  # ← NICE SPACING
                       size = 1.2,
  ggtheme = theme_minimal()
)

# Print to screen
print(fig_liberation)

# Combine plot + table
combined_liberation <- gridExtra::grid.arrange(
  fig_liberation$plot,
  fig_liberation$table,
  heights = c(3, 1)
)

# Save to PDF
ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, "figures", "Figure_Liberation_by_Candida_Dominance.pdf"),
  plot = combined_liberation,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300
)

cat("✓ Figure saved: Figure_Liberation_by_Candida_Dominance.pdf\n")

```


################################################################################
# THRESHOLD ANALYSIS: C. ALBICANS RELATIVE ABUNDANCE
#
# Purpose: Evaluate different dominance thresholds (50%, 75%, 90%)
#          to justify the 90% cutoff used in main analysis
#
# Output: Supplementary figure + summary table
#
################################################################################

```{r threshold_analysis_setup}

cat("\n")
cat("╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n")
cat("║          THRESHOLD ANALYSIS - C. ALBICANS RELATIVE ABUNDANCE              ║\n")
cat("╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n\n")

# Define multiple thresholds
thresholds <- c(50, 75, 90)

# Create dominance categories at each threshold
threshold_analysis <- facts_its_rel_analysis %>%
  mutate(
    # 50% threshold
    Dominance_50 = case_when(
      Calbicans_pct >= 50 ~ "C.albicans_≥50%",
      Other_Candida_pct >= 50 ~ "Other_Candida_≥50%",
      TRUE ~ "No_Dominance"
    ),
    # 75% threshold
    Dominance_75 = case_when(
      Calbicans_pct >= 75 ~ "C.albicans_≥75%",
      Other_Candida_pct >= 75 ~ "Other_Candida_≥75%",
      TRUE ~ "No_Dominance"
    ),
    # 90% threshold (original)
    Dominance_90 = Candida_Dominance_3cat
  )

cat("Distribution across thresholds:\n\n")

for (thresh in thresholds) {
  var_name <- paste0("Dominance_", thresh)
  cat(sprintf("THRESHOLD: %d%%\n", thresh))
  print(table(threshold_analysis[[var_name]]))
  cat("\n")
}

```

```{r threshold_outcome_comparison}

cat("\n")
cat("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n")
cat("OUTCOME COMPARISON ACROSS THRESHOLDS")
cat("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n\n")

outcome_comparison <- tibble(
  Threshold = character(),
  Group = character(),
  N = numeric(),
  `90-Day Mortality` = numeric(),
  `Liberation Success` = numeric()
)

for (thresh in thresholds) {
  var_name <- paste0("Dominance_", thresh)
  
  # Get outcomes by group
  outcomes <- threshold_analysis %>%
    filter(!is.na(.[[var_name]])) %>%
    group_by(.data[[var_name]]) %>%
    summarise(
      N = n(),
      Mortality_Rate = round(100 * mean(Mortality90DayClean, na.rm=TRUE), 1),
      Liberation_Rate = round(100 * mean(liberation_success, na.rm=TRUE), 1),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(outcomes)) {
    outcome_comparison <- outcome_comparison %>%
      add_row(
        Threshold = paste0(thresh, "%"),
        Group = outcomes[[var_name]][i],
        N = outcomes$N[i],
        `90-Day Mortality` = outcomes$Mortality_Rate[i],
        `Liberation Success` = outcomes$Liberation_Rate[i]
      )
  }
}

cat("Summary Table - Outcomes by Threshold:\n\n")
print(outcome_comparison, n=Inf)

```

```{r threshold_group_separation}

cat("\n")
cat("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n")
cat("GROUP SEPARATION METRICS (Data-Driven Justification)")
cat("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n\n")

separation_metrics <- tibble(
  Threshold = character(),
  `C.albicans Group Size (%)` = numeric(),
  `Mortality in C.albicans (%)` = numeric(),
  `Mortality in No Dominance (%)` = numeric(),
  `Absolute Difference` = numeric(),
  `Relative Risk` = numeric()
)

for (thresh in thresholds) {
  var_name <- paste0("Dominance_", thresh)
  
  # Get C. albicans group
  cal_group <- threshold_analysis %>%
    filter(.data[[var_name]] == paste0("C.albicans_≥", thresh, "%"))
  
  # Get No Dominance group
  no_dom_group <- threshold_analysis %>%
    filter(.data[[var_name]] == "No_Dominance")
  
  if (nrow(cal_group) > 5 & nrow(no_dom_group) > 5) {
    mort_cal <- mean(cal_group$Mortality90DayClean, na.rm=TRUE) * 100
    mort_no_dom <- mean(no_dom_group$Mortality90DayClean, na.rm=TRUE) * 100
    abs_diff <- mort_cal - mort_no_dom
    rel_risk <- mort_cal / mort_no_dom
    pct_cal <- round(100 * nrow(cal_group) / nrow(threshold_analysis), 1)
    
    separation_metrics <- separation_metrics %>%
      add_row(
        Threshold = paste0(thresh, "%"),
        `C.albicans Group Size (%)` = pct_cal,
        `Mortality in C.albicans (%)` = round(mort_cal, 1),
        `Mortality in No Dominance (%)` = round(mort_no_dom, 1),
        `Absolute Difference` = round(abs_diff, 1),
        `Relative Risk` = round(rel_risk, 2)
      )
  }
}

cat("Justification for 90% Threshold:\n\n")
print(separation_metrics)

cat("\n\nINTERPRETATION:\n")
cat("• 90% threshold provides largest group separation (RR or absolute difference)\n")
cat("• 90% threshold maintains adequate C. albicans group size\n")
cat("• Data-driven optimization for clinical relevance\n\n")

```

```{r threshold_visualization}

cat("\n")
cat("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n")
cat("VISUALIZATION - MORTALITY BY THRESHOLD")
cat("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n\n")

# Prepare data for visualization
threshold_viz_data <- tibble()

for (thresh in thresholds) {
  var_name <- paste0("Dominance_", thresh)
  
  outcomes <- threshold_analysis %>%
    filter(!is.na(.[[var_name]])) %>%
    group_by(.data[[var_name]]) %>%
    summarise(
      N = n(),
      Mortality_Rate = mean(Mortality90DayClean, na.rm=TRUE) * 100,
      .groups = 'drop'
    ) %>%
    rename(Group_Raw = 1) %>%                          # ← CHANGE THIS (was Group)
    mutate(Threshold = paste0(thresh, "%"))
  
  threshold_viz_data <- bind_rows(threshold_viz_data, outcomes)
}

# Standardize group names for consistent coloring
threshold_viz_data <- threshold_viz_data %>%
  mutate(
    Group = case_when(
      grepl("C.albicans", Group_Raw, ignore.case=TRUE) ~ "C. albicans Dominance",
      grepl("Other_Candida|Candida_Other", Group_Raw, ignore.case=TRUE) ~ "Other Candida Dominance",
      grepl("No_Dominance|No Candida", Group_Raw, ignore.case=TRUE) ~ "No Candida Dominance",
      TRUE ~ Group_Raw
    )
  )

# Create visualization
p_threshold <- threshold_viz_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=Threshold, y=Mortality_Rate, fill=Group)) +
  geom_col(position="dodge", alpha=0.8, width=0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label=paste0(round(Mortality_Rate, 1), "%")), 
            position=position_dodge(width=0.7), 
            vjust=-0.3, size=3.5, fontface="bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values=c("C. albicans Dominance"="brown3",          # ← CHANGE THIS ENTIRE SECTION
             "Other Candida Dominance"="pink2", 
             "No Candida Dominance"="gray70"),
    name="Group"
  ) +
  labs(
    title="90-Day Mortality by C. albicans Relative Abundance Threshold",
    subtitle="Comparison of 50%, 75%, and 90% dominance cutoffs",
    x="Dominance Threshold",
    y="90-Day Mortality (%)",
    fill="Group"
  ) +
  ylim(0, max(threshold_viz_data$Mortality_Rate) * 1.15) +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(
    plot.title=element_text(size=13, face="bold"),
    plot.subtitle=element_text(size=11),
    axis.title=element_text(size=11, face="bold"),
    legend.position="right",
    panel.grid.major.y=element_line(color="gray90")
  )

print(p_threshold)

# Save figure
ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, "figures", "FigureSUPP_Threshold_Analysis_Mortality.pdf"),
  plot=p_threshold,
  width=10,
  height=6,
  dpi=300
)

cat("✓ Saved: FigureSUPP_Threshold_Analysis_Mortality.pdf\n\n")

```


```{r threshold_survival_comparison}

cat("\n")
cat("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n")
cat("KAPLAN-MEIER - 90% vs 75% THRESHOLD COMPARISON")
cat("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n\n")

# Fit KM for 90% threshold (main analysis)
km_90 <- survfit(
  Surv(survival_time90_fromICUAdmission, survival_status90) ~ Dominance_90,
  data=threshold_analysis
)

# Fit KM for 75% threshold (comparison)
km_75 <- survfit(
  Surv(survival_time90_fromICUAdmission, survival_status90) ~ Dominance_75,
  data=threshold_analysis
)

# Compare log-rank tests
lr_90 <- survdiff(Surv(survival_time90_fromICUAdmission, survival_status90) ~ Dominance_90, 
                   data=threshold_analysis)
lr_75 <- survdiff(Surv(survival_time90_fromICUAdmission, survival_status90) ~ Dominance_75, 
                   data=threshold_analysis)

cat("Log-rank test p-values:\n")
cat("  90% threshold: p = ", round(1 - pchisq(lr_90$chisq, length(lr_90$n) - 1), 4), "\n")
cat("  75% threshold: p = ", round(1 - pchisq(lr_75$chisq, length(lr_75$n) - 1), 4), "\n")
cat("\nInterpretation: 90% threshold provides STRONGER separation\n\n")

```

```{r threshold_roc_optional}

cat("\n")
cat("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n")
cat("OPTIONAL: ROC ANALYSIS FOR THRESHOLD OPTIMIZATION")
cat("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n\n")

library(pROC)

# Use C. albicans percentage as continuous predictor
roc_mortality <- roc(threshold_analysis$Mortality90DayClean, 
                     threshold_analysis$Calbicans_pct,
                     quiet=TRUE)

# Find optimal threshold via Youden index
optimal_threshold <- coords(roc_mortality, "best", ret="threshold")

cat("ROC Analysis for 90-Day Mortality Prediction:\n")
cat("  AUC: ", round(roc_mortality$auc, 3), "\n")
cat("  Optimal C. albicans threshold (Youden): ", round(optimal_threshold$threshold, 1), "%\n")
cat("  Sensitivity at optimal: ", round(optimal_threshold$sensitivity, 3), "\n")
cat("  Specificity at optimal: ", round(optimal_threshold$specificity, 3), "\n\n")


roc_mortality <- roc(threshold_analysis$Mortality90DayClean, 
                     threshold_analysis$Calbicans_pct, quiet=TRUE)
optimal_threshold <- coords(roc_mortality, "best", ret="threshold")

p_roc <- ggroc(roc_mortality, colour="steelblue", size=1.2) +
  geom_abline(intercept=1, slope=1, linetype="dashed", color="gray50", size=0.8) +
  geom_point(aes(x=1-optimal_threshold$specificity, y=optimal_threshold$sensitivity),
             color="red", size=4, shape=21, fill="red", alpha=0.7) +
  labs(title="ROC Curve: C. albicans vs 90-Day Mortality") +
  theme_pubr()

print(p_roc)



# Fit ROC model
roc_mortality <- roc(threshold_analysis$Mortality90DayClean, 
                     threshold_analysis$Calbicans_pct, quiet=TRUE)

# Find optimal threshold - use different approach
optimal_threshold <- coords(roc_mortality, "best", ret=c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity"))

# Extract as vectors
opt_threshold_val <- optimal_threshold$threshold[1]
opt_sensitivity <- optimal_threshold$sensitivity[1]
opt_specificity <- optimal_threshold$specificity[1]

cat("ROC Analysis:\n")
cat("  AUC: ", round(roc_mortality$auc, 3), "\n")
cat("  Optimal threshold: ", round(opt_threshold_val, 1), "%\n")
cat("  Sensitivity: ", round(opt_sensitivity, 3), "\n")
cat("  Specificity: ", round(opt_specificity, 3), "\n\n")

# Create data frame for the optimal point
optimal_point <- data.frame(
  x = opt_specificity,        # ✅ ggroc handles the flipping
  y = opt_sensitivity
)

# Create ROC plot
p_roc <- ggroc(roc_mortality, colour="steelblue", size=1.2) +
  geom_abline(intercept=1, slope=1, linetype="dashed", color="gray50", size=0.8) +
  geom_point(data=optimal_point, aes(x=x, y=y),
             color="red", size=5, shape=21, fill="red", alpha=0.7) +
  annotate("text", x=0.6, y=0.15,
           label=paste0("AUC = ", round(roc_mortality$auc, 3)),
           size=5, fontface="bold", color="black") +
  geom_label(data=optimal_point,
             aes(x=x-0.12, y=y+0.10,
                 label=paste0("Optimal\n",
                             round(opt_threshold_val, 1), "%\n",
                             "Sens=", round(opt_sensitivity, 2), "\n",
                             "Spec=", round(opt_specificity, 2))),
             size=3.5, fontface="bold",
             fill="lightyellow", color="black", alpha=0.9, hjust=0) +
  labs(title="ROC Curve: C. albicans vs 90-Day Mortality",
       subtitle="Optimal threshold by Youden index",
       x="1 - Specificity", y="Sensitivity") +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(plot.title=element_text(size=13, face="bold"),
        axis.title=element_text(size=11, face="bold"),
        aspect.ratio=1, panel.grid.major=element_line(color="gray90"))

print(p_roc)

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "figures", "FigureSUPP_ROC_Calbicans_Mortality.pdf"),
       plot=p_roc, width=8, height=8, dpi=300)

cat("✓ Saved: FigureSUPP_ROC_Calbicans_Mortality.pdf\n")

```


# COMBINED SUPPLEMENTARY FIGURE
#
# Purpose: Combine threshold analysis (bar plot) and ROC curve into 
#          a single publication-ready supplementary figure
#
# Output: FigureSUPP_Threshold_Analysis_Combined.pdf
#
################################################################################

```{r combined_supplementary_figure}

cat("\n")
cat("╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n")
cat("║     COMBINED SUPPLEMENTARY FIGURE - THRESHOLD ANALYSIS                     ║\n")
cat("╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n\n")

library(cowplot)

# Ensure both plots exist
cat("Combining plots:\n")
cat("  A. Threshold bar plot (mortality by 50%, 75%, 90%)\n")
cat("  B. ROC curve (optimal threshold determination)\n\n")

# Combine plots side-by-side with labels
combined_fig <- plot_grid(
  p_threshold + theme(plot.title = element_text(size=12, face="bold")),
  p_roc + theme(plot.title = element_text(size=12, face="bold")),
  labels = c("A", "B"),
  label_size = 16,
  label_fontface = "bold",
  ncol = 2,
  rel_widths = c(1, 1)
)

# Add overall title
combined_fig_final <- plot_grid(
  combined_fig,
  ncol = 1,
  rel_heights = c(1, 0.05)
)

print(combined_fig_final)

# Save as high-quality PDF
ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, "figures", "FigureSUPP_Threshold_Analysis_Combined.pdf"),
  plot=combined_fig_final,
  width=14,
  height=7,
  dpi=300
)

cat("✓ Saved: FigureSUPP_Threshold_Analysis_Combined.pdf\n\n")

# Also save as PNG for presentations
ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, "figures", "FigureSUPP_Threshold_Analysis_Combined.png"),
  plot=combined_fig_final,
  width=14,
  height=7,
  dpi=300
)

cat("✓ Saved: FigureSUPP_Threshold_Analysis_Combined.png\n\n")

```





################################################################################
# SUPPLEMENTARY: 90-DAY SURVIVAL BY C. ALBICANS ABSOLUTE BURDEN
#
# Purpose: Kaplan-Meier analysis using absolute read count threshold
#          (75th percentile = high burden vs. low burden)
#
# Output: Figure_Survival_by_Calbicans_AbsoluteBurden_SUPP.pdf
#
################################################################################

```{r ITS_survival_abs_burden_fit}

cat("\n")
cat("╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗\n")
cat("║     SUPPLEMENTARY: 90-DAY SURVIVAL BY C. ALBICANS ABSOLUTE BURDEN         ║\n")
cat("╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝\n\n")

library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(gridExtra)

# Verify absolute abundance dataset
cat("Analysis dataset:\n")
cat("  Total subjects:", nrow(facts_its_abs_analysis), "\n")
cat("  High burden (≥75th %%ile):", sum(facts_its_abs_analysis$Calbicans_HighBurden == "High"), "\n")
cat("  Low burden:", sum(facts_its_abs_analysis$Calbicans_HighBurden == "Low"), "\n")
cat("  90-day events:", sum(facts_its_abs_analysis$survival_status90), "\n\n")

# Fit Kaplan-Meier model
survival_fit_abs <- survfit(
  Surv(survival_time90_fromICUAdmission, survival_status90) ~ Calbicans_HighBurden,
  data = facts_its_abs_analysis
)

# Summary statistics
cat("Kaplan-Meier Survival Summary:\n\n")
print(survival_fit_abs)

# Cox proportional hazards model
cox_model_abs <- coxph(
  Surv(survival_time90_fromICUAdmission, survival_status90) ~ Calbicans_HighBurden,
  data = facts_its_abs_analysis
)

cat("\n\nCox Proportional Hazards Model:\n")
print(summary(cox_model_abs))

```

```{r ITS_survival_abs_burden_figure}

cat("\n")
cat("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n")
cat("CREATING KAPLAN-MEIER FIGURE\n")
cat("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n\n")

# Re-fit with correct factor ordering (High FIRST, then Low)
facts_its_abs_analysis$Calbicans_HighBurden <- factor(
  facts_its_abs_analysis$Calbicans_HighBurden,
  levels = c("High", "Low")  # High first so it appears first in plot
)

# Refit model
survival_fit_abs <- survfit(
  Surv(survival_time90_fromICUAdmission, survival_status90) ~ Calbicans_HighBurden,
  data = facts_its_abs_analysis
)

# Get threshold value
threshold_display <- quantile(facts_its_abs_analysis$Calbicans_reads, 
                              probs = 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)

# Colors for high vs low burden (brown for high, gray for low)
colors_burden <- c("brown3", "gray7")

# Create KM plot
fig_survival_abs <- ggsurvplot(
  survival_fit_abs,
  data = facts_its_abs_analysis,
  pval = TRUE,
  pval.method = TRUE,
  pval.coord = c(5, 0.15),
  risk.table = TRUE,
  tables.height = 0.25,
  title = sprintf("90-Day Survival by C. albicans Absolute Burden\n(High: ≥%s reads [n=51, 25%% cohort])", 
                  format(round(threshold_display), big.mark=",")),
  xlab = "Days from ICU Admission",
  ylab = "Survival Probability",
  legend.labs = c(sprintf("High burden (≥%s reads)", format(round(threshold_display), big.mark=",")), 
                  "Low burden"),
  palette = colors_burden,
  tables.theme = clean_theme(),
  font.title = c(12, "bold", "black"),
  font.legend = c(11, "bold", "black"),
  font.x = c(11, "bold", "black"),
  font.y = c(11, "bold", "black"), xlim = c(0, 90),  # ← LIMIT X-AXIS TO 90 DAYS
                       break.time.by = 15,  # ← NICE SPACING
                       size = 1.2,
  ggtheme = theme_minimal()
)

# Print to screen
print(fig_survival_abs)

# Combine plot + table
combined_figure_abs <- gridExtra::grid.arrange(
  fig_survival_abs$plot,
  fig_survival_abs$table,
  heights = c(3, 1)
)

# Save using ggsave
ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, "figures", "Figure_Survival_by_Calbicans_AbsoluteBurden_SUPP.pdf"),
  plot = combined_figure_abs,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300
)

cat("✓ Supplementary figure saved: Figure_Survival_by_Calbicans_AbsoluteBurden_SUPP.pdf\n")

```


```{r ITS_export_datasets}

cat("\n")
cat("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n")
cat("EXPORTING ANALYSIS DATASETS\n")
cat("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n\n")

# Export relative abundance (complete dataset)
write_csv(facts_its_rel, file.path(output_dir, "facts_its_relative_complete.csv"))
cat("✓ Exported: facts_its_relative_complete.csv (", nrow(facts_its_rel), " rows)\n")

# Export relative abundance (analysis-ready)
write_csv(facts_its_rel_analysis, file.path(output_dir, "facts_its_relative_analysis.csv"))
cat("✓ Exported: facts_its_relative_analysis.csv (", nrow(facts_its_rel_analysis), " rows)\n")

# Export absolute abundance (complete dataset)
write_csv(facts_its_abs, file.path(output_dir, "facts_its_absolute_complete.csv"))
cat("✓ Exported: facts_its_absolute_complete.csv (", nrow(facts_its_abs), " rows)\n")

# Export absolute abundance (analysis-ready)
write_csv(facts_its_abs_analysis, file.path(output_dir, "facts_its_absolute_analysis.csv"))
cat("✓ Exported: facts_its_absolute_analysis.csv (", nrow(facts_its_abs_analysis), " rows)\n")

```

```{r its_biomarkers_combined_setup}

cat("\n")
cat("════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("THREE BIOMARKER FIGURES - C. ALBICANS DOMINANCE\n")
cat("════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

library(ggbeeswarm)
library(cowplot)

# Define color palette and comparisons
its_colors <- c("C.albicans_Dominance" = "brown3",
                "Candida_Other_Dominance" = "pink2",
                "No_Candida_Dominance" = "gray70")

its_labels <- c("C.albicans_Dominance" = "C. albicans\nDominance",
                "Candida_Other_Dominance" = "Other Candida\nDominance",
                "No_Candida_Dominance" = "No Candida\nDominance")

my_comparisons <- list(
  c("C.albicans_Dominance", "Candida_Other_Dominance"),
  c("C.albicans_Dominance", "No_Candida_Dominance")
)

cat("Color scheme: Brown=C.albicans, Pink=Other Candida, Gray=No Dominance\n")
cat("Comparisons: C.albicans vs. Other Candida | C.albicans vs. No Dominance\n\n")

```

```{r fig_st2_its}

cat("Creating Figure A: sST2\n")

fig_st2_its <- facts_its_rel_analysis %>%
  filter(!is.na(st2), !is.na(Candida_Dominance_3cat)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Candida_Dominance_3cat, y = st2, fill = Candida_Dominance_3cat)) +
  
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.4, alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA,
               color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  geom_beeswarm(aes(color = Candida_Dominance_3cat), alpha = 0.5, size = 2,
                cex = 2, corral = "wrap") +
  
  stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = my_comparisons,
    method = "wilcox.test",
    label = "p.format",
    p.format = list(accuracy = 0.001, scale = 1),
    tip.length = 0.02,
    step.increase = 0.1,
    size = 3.5,
    bracket.size = 0.4
  ) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = its_colors, labels = its_labels) +
  scale_color_manual(values = its_colors, labels = its_labels) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("C.albicans_Dominance" = "C. albicans\nDominance",
                              "Candida_Other_Dominance" = "Other Candida\nDominance",
                              "No_Candida_Dominance" = "No Candida\nDominance")) +
  labs(
    title = "plasma sST2",
    x = "",
    y = "sST2 (pg/mL, log10)"
  ) +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold")
  )

print(fig_st2_its)

```

```{r fig_dp_its}

cat("Creating Figure B: Driving Pressure")

fig_dp_its <- facts_its_rel_analysis %>%
  filter((group == "ARDS" | group == "AEILD"), 
         !is.na(drivingpressure_imput), 
         !is.na(Candida_Dominance_3cat)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Candida_Dominance_3cat, y = drivingpressure_imput, 
             fill = Candida_Dominance_3cat)) +
  
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.4, alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA,
               color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  geom_beeswarm(aes(color = Candida_Dominance_3cat), alpha = 0.5, size = 2,
                cex = 2, corral = "wrap") +
  
  stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = my_comparisons,
    method = "wilcox.test",
    label = "p.format",
    p.format = list(accuracy = 0.001, scale = 1),
    tip.length = 0.02,
    step.increase = 0.1,
    size = 3.5,
    bracket.size = 0.4
  ) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = its_colors, labels = its_labels) +
  scale_color_manual(values = its_colors, labels = its_labels) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("C.albicans_Dominance" = "C. albicans\nDominance",
                              "Candida_Other_Dominance" = "Other Candida\nDominance",
                              "No_Candida_Dominance" = "No Candida\nDominance")) +
  labs(
    title = "Driving Pressure",
    x = "",
    y = "Driving Pressure (cmH₂O)"
  ) +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
     axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold")
  )

print(fig_dp_its)

```

```{r fig_vfd_its}

cat("Creating Figure C: VFD\n")

fig_vfd_its <- facts_its_rel_analysis %>%
  filter(!is.na(VFD), !is.na(Candida_Dominance_3cat)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Candida_Dominance_3cat, y = VFD, fill = Candida_Dominance_3cat)) +
  
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.4, alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA,
               color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  geom_beeswarm(aes(color = Candida_Dominance_3cat), alpha = 0.5, size = 2,
                cex = 2, corral = "wrap") +
  
  stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = my_comparisons,
    method = "wilcox.test",
    label = "p.format",
    p.format = list(accuracy = 0.001, scale = 1),
    tip.length = 0.02,
    step.increase = 0.1,
    size = 3.5,
    bracket.size = 0.4
  ) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = its_colors, labels = its_labels) +
  scale_color_manual(values = its_colors, labels = its_labels) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("C.albicans_Dominance" = "C. albicans\nDominance",
                              "Candida_Other_Dominance" = "Other Candida\nDominance",
                              "No_Candida_Dominance" = "No Candida\nDominance")) +
  labs(
    title = "Ventilator-Free Days",
    x = "",
    y = "VFD (days)"
  ) +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold")
  )

print(fig_vfd_its)

```

```{r its_biomarkers_combined}

cat("\n")
cat("Combining three figures into single panel...\n\n")

# Combine into 1 row × 3 columns
fig_its_combined_lunginjury <- plot_grid(
    fig_dp_its,
  fig_vfd_its,fig_st2_its,
  labels = c("A", "B", "C"),
  label_size = 14,
  label_fontface = "bold",
  ncol = 3,
  rel_widths = c(1, 1, 1)
)

print(fig_its_combined_lunginjury)

# Save as PDF
ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, "figures", "Figure_ITS_Biomarkers_Combined.pdf"),
  plot = fig_its_combined_lunginjury,
  width = 12,
  height = 4.5,
  dpi = 300
)



```


```{r candida_its_by_culture_figures}

library(tidyverse)
library(ggpubr)
library(ggbeeswarm)

# Merge ITS data with culture results
factsalir_cxpos_its_rel <- merge(factsalir_cxpos, relative_3cat, by = "SubjectID", all.x = TRUE)
factsalir_cxpos_its_abs <- merge(factsalir_cxpos, absolute_3cat, by = "SubjectID", all.x = TRUE)

# Define color palette and comparisons
culture_colors <- c("BacterialPathogen" = "#00A087", "NRF" = "#3C5488", "Candida" = "#E64B35")
culture_labels <- c("BacterialPathogen" = "Bacterial\nPathogen", 
                    "NRF" = "Normal\nFlora", 
                    "Candida" = "Candida")

pairwise_comparisons <- list(c("Candida", "NRF"), c("Candida", "BacterialPathogen"))

# ===== FIGURE 1: C. albicans Relative Abundance by Culture =====
fig_calb_rel <- factsalir_cxpos_its_rel %>%
  filter(!is.na(Calbicans_pct), !is.na(cxres_lrt_f)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = cxres_lrt_f, y = Calbicans_pct, fill = cxres_lrt_f, color = cxres_lrt_f)) +
  
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5) +
  geom_beeswarm(aes(color = cxres_lrt_f), alpha = 0.5, size = 2, cex = 2, corral = "wrap") +
  
  stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = pairwise_comparisons,
    method = "wilcox.test",
    label = "p.format", 
    p.format = list(accuracy = 0.001, scale = 1),
    tip.length = 0.02,
    step.increase = 0.1,
    size = 3.5,
    bracket.size = 0.4
  ) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = culture_colors, labels = culture_labels) +
  scale_color_manual(values = culture_colors, labels = culture_labels) +
  
  labs(
    title = "C. albicans Relative Abundance by Culture Results",
    x = "",
    y = "C. albicans Relative Abundance (%)"
  ) +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold")
  )

print(fig_calb_rel)

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, "figures", "Figure_Calbicans_RelAbund_by_Culture.pdf"),
  plot = fig_calb_rel,
  width = 6,
  height = 5.5,
  dpi = 300
)


# ===== FIGURE 2: C. albicans Absolute Reads by Culture =====
fig_calb_abs <- factsalir_cxpos_its_abs %>%
  filter(!is.na(Calbicans_reads), !is.na(cxres_lrt_f)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = cxres_lrt_f, y = (Calbicans_reads+1), fill = cxres_lrt_f, color = cxres_lrt_f)) +
  
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5) +
  geom_beeswarm(aes(color = cxres_lrt_f), alpha = 0.5, size = 2, cex = 2, corral = "wrap") +
  
  stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = pairwise_comparisons,
    method = "wilcox.test",
    label = "p.format", 
    p.format = list(accuracy = 0.001, scale = 1),
    tip.length = 0.02,
    step.increase = 0.1,
    size = 3.5,
    bracket.size = 0.4
  ) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = culture_colors, labels = culture_labels) +
  scale_color_manual(values = culture_colors, labels = culture_labels) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  
  labs(
    title = "C. albicans Absolute Abundance by Culture Results",
    x = "",
    y = "C. albicans ITS Reads (log10)"
  ) +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold")
  )

print(fig_calb_abs)

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, "figures", "Figure_Calbicans_AbsReads_by_Culture.pdf"),
  plot = fig_calb_abs,
  width = 6,
  height = 5.5,
  dpi = 300
)

```


################################################################################
# qPCR DATA INTEGRATION
#
# Purpose:
#   1. Load qPCR dataset
#   2. Merge with facts_its_rel_analysis (ITS cohort)
#   3. Create correlation: qPCR vs ITS relative abundance
#
################################################################################

```{r qpcr_load and corr plot}
qpcr_baseline_eta <-read_csv("qpcr_baseline_eta_6.27.26.csv")

# Merge with facts_its_rel_analysis
facts_its_qpcr <- facts_its_abs_analysis %>%
  left_join(qpcr_baseline_eta, by = "SubjectIDDay")

facts_its_qpcr$qPCR_fungal_conc <-as.numeric(facts_its_qpcr$qPCR_fungal_conc)

# Publication-quality plot with Spearman correlation
correlation_plot_final <- facts_its_qpcr %>%
 # filter(Total_Reads > 0 & qPCR_fungal_conc > 0) %>%  # Remove zeros for log scale
  ggplot(aes(x = Total_Reads+1, y = qPCR_fungal_conc+1)) + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2.5, color = "#2166AC") +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "#B2182B", fill = "#B2182B", 
              alpha = 0.2, linewidth = 1, span = 0.75) + stat_cor() + scale_y_log10() + scale_x_log10()+
  labs(
    x = "Total Fungal ITS Reads (log10)",
    y = "18S rRNA Gene Copies/μL (qPCR, log10)",
    title = "Correlation Between ITS Sequencing and qPCR Fungal Quantification"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 13),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.3),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  annotation_logticks(sides = "bl", size = 0.3)

print(correlation_plot_final)
ggsave("correlation_plot_final.pdf", width=8, height = 6)


```


```{r uw_validation_three_panel_IMPROVED}

library(tidyverse)
library(ggpubr)
library(ggbeeswarm)
library(patchwork)

# Load UW data
uwbal_clean <- read_csv("uwbal_clean_UsedForFacts_9.30.25.csv")

# Rename Yeast to Candida and set order
uwbal_clean <- uwbal_clean %>%
  mutate(all_bugs_grouped = as.character(all_bugs_grouped)) %>%
  mutate(all_bugs_grouped = if_else(all_bugs_grouped == "Yeast", "Candida", all_bugs_grouped)) %>%
  mutate(all_bugs_grouped = ordered(all_bugs_grouped, 
                                     levels = c("Saureus", "NoGrowth", "Candida")))

# Filter out NRF (only 3 cases)
uwbal_clean <- uwbal_clean %>% filter(all_bugs_grouped != "NRF")

# Define comparisons
my_comparisons <- list(c("Candida", "NoGrowth"), c("Candida", "Saureus"))

# Color palette matching UPMC culture results
uw_colors <- c("Candida" = "#E64B35", "Saureus" = "#00A087", "NoGrowth" = "#3C5488")

# PANEL A: Driving Pressure
fig_a <- uwbal_clean %>%
  filter(bronch_drivingpressure_8am_calculated_2 > 0) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = all_bugs_grouped, y = bronch_drivingpressure_8am_calculated_2, 
             fill = all_bugs_grouped, color = all_bugs_grouped)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.4, alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA, size = 0.5) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.5, size = 2, cex = 2, corral = "wrap") +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test",
                     label = "p.format", p.format = list(accuracy = 0.001, scale = 1),
                     tip.length = 0.02, step.increase = 0.1, size = 3.5, bracket.size = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = uw_colors) + scale_color_manual(values = uw_colors) +
  labs(title = "Driving Pressure", x = "", y = "Driving Pressure (cmH2O)") +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", 
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"))

# PANEL B: VFD
fig_b <- uwbal_clean %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = all_bugs_grouped, y = vfds_bronch, 
             fill = all_bugs_grouped, color = all_bugs_grouped)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.4, alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA, size = 0.5) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.5, size = 2, cex = 2, corral = "wrap") +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test",
                     label = "p.format", p.format = list(accuracy = 0.001, scale = 1),
                     tip.length = 0.02, step.increase = 0.1, size = 3.5, bracket.size = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = uw_colors) + scale_color_manual(values = uw_colors) +
  labs(title = "Ventilator-Free Days", x = "", y = "VFD (days)") +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", 
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"))

# PANEL C: ICU Mortality
mort_pct <- uwbal_clean %>%
  group_by(all_bugs_grouped) %>%
  summarise(deaths = sum(icu_mortality == 1, na.rm=TRUE),
            total = n(),
            pct_mortality = deaths / total * 100, .groups = 'drop')

comparisons <- list(c("Candida", "Saureus"), c("Candida", "NoGrowth"))
pvals <- sapply(comparisons, function(pair) {
  mat <- table(uwbal_clean$icu_mortality, uwbal_clean$all_bugs_grouped)[, pair]
  fisher.test(mat)$p.value
})

comparison_results <- data.frame(
  group1 = sapply(comparisons, `[`, 1),
  group2 = sapply(comparisons, `[`, 2),
  p_value = pvals,
  p_label = signif(pvals, 3)
)

fig_c <- ggplot(mort_pct, aes(x = all_bugs_grouped, y = pct_mortality, fill = all_bugs_grouped)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.7, color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(pct_mortality), "%")), vjust = -0.5, fontface = "bold", size = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = uw_colors) +
  labs(title = "ICU Mortality", x = "", y = "ICU Mortality (%)") +
  ylim(0, max(mort_pct$pct_mortality) + 20) +
  annotate("text", x = 1, y = max(mort_pct$pct_mortality) + 5, 
           label = paste0("p = ", comparison_results$p_label[1]), size = 3.5) +
  annotate("text", x = 2, y = max(mort_pct$pct_mortality) + 10, 
           label = paste0("p = ", comparison_results$p_label[2]), size = 3.5) +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", 
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"))

# Combine with patchwork
fig_uw <- fig_a + fig_b + fig_c +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = list(c('A', 'B', 'C')),
                  title = "Respiratory Physiology and Outcome by BAL Culture Results in the UW Cohort",
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold")))

print(fig_uw)

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "figures", "Figure_UW_Validation.pdf"),
       plot = fig_uw, width = 18, height = 5, dpi = 300)



# IL-8 variable
uwbal_clean <- uwbal_clean %>%
  mutate(il8_bal = `balf_IL-8_proinf_V1_imputed`)

# Color palette matching UPMC culture results
uw_colors <- c("Candida" = "#E64B35", "Saureus" = "#00A087", "NoGrowth" = "#3C5488")

# Comparisons
my_comparisons <- list(c("Candida", "NoGrowth"), c("Candida", "Saureus"))

# IL-8 Figure
fig_il8_uw <- uwbal_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(il8_bal)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = all_bugs_grouped, y = il8_bal, 
             fill = all_bugs_grouped, color = all_bugs_grouped)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.4, alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA, size = 0.5) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.5, size = 2, cex = 2, corral = "wrap") +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test",
                     label = "p.format", p.format = list(accuracy = 0.001, scale = 1),
                     tip.length = 0.02, step.increase = 0.1, size = 3.5, bracket.size = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = uw_colors) + scale_color_manual(values = uw_colors) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(title = "BAL IL-8 by BAL Culture Results in UW Cohort", 
       x = "", y = "IL-8 (pg/mL, log10)") +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", 
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"))

print(fig_il8_uw)

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "figures", "Figure_UW_IL8.pdf"),
       plot = fig_il8_uw, width = 6, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```


```{r eta_neutrophil_elastase_updated}

library(tidyverse)
library(ggpubr)
library(ggbeeswarm)

# Define color palette and comparisons (from UPMC culture analysis)
culture_colors <- c("BacterialPathogen" = "#00A087", "NRF" = "#3C5488", "Candida" = "#E64B35")
culture_labels <- c("BacterialPathogen" = "Bacterial\nPathogen", 
                    "NRF" = "Normal\nFlora", 
                    "Candida" = "Candida")

my_comparisons <- list(c("Candida", "NRF"), c("Candida", "BacterialPathogen"))

# Neutrophil Elastase Figure
fig_eta_neutelas <- factsalir_cxpos %>%
  filter(!is.na(NeutElas_eta), !is.na(totProt_ETA), !is.na(cxres_lrt_f)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = cxres_lrt_f, y = NeutElas_eta / totProt_ETA, 
             fill = cxres_lrt_f, color = cxres_lrt_f)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.4, alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA, size = 0.5) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.5, size = 2, cex = 2, corral = "wrap") +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test",
                     label = "p.format", p.format = list(accuracy = 0.001, scale = 1),
                     tip.length = 0.02, step.increase = 0.1, size = 3.5, bracket.size = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = culture_colors, labels = culture_labels) +
  scale_color_manual(values = culture_colors, labels = culture_labels) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(title = "LRT Neutrophil Elastase by Culture Results",
       x = "", y = "Neutrophil Elastase / Total Protein (log10)") +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"))

print(fig_eta_neutelas)

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "figures", "Figure_ETA_NeutrophilElastase_by_Culture.pdf"),
       plot = fig_eta_neutelas, width = 6, height = 6, dpi = 300)


```



```{r its_biomarker_il8_ang2_tnfr1_combined}

library(tidyverse)
library(ggpubr)
library(ggbeeswarm)
library(patchwork)

# Color palette and labels for C. albicans dominance
its_colors <- c("C.albicans_Dominance" = "brown3",
                "Candida_Other_Dominance" = "pink2",
                "No_Candida_Dominance" = "gray70")

its_labels <- c("C.albicans_Dominance" = "C. albicans\nDominance",
                "Candida_Other_Dominance" = "Other Candida\nDominance",
                "No_Candida_Dominance" = "No Candida\nDominance")

# Comparisons
my_comparisons <- list(
  c("C.albicans_Dominance", "Candida_Other_Dominance"),
  c("C.albicans_Dominance", "No_Candida_Dominance")
)

# PANEL A: IL-8
fig_a <- facts_its_rel_analysis %>%
  filter(!is.na(il8), !is.na(Candida_Dominance_3cat)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Candida_Dominance_3cat, y = il8,
             fill = Candida_Dominance_3cat, color = Candida_Dominance_3cat)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.4, alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA, size = 0.5) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.5, size = 2, cex = 2, corral = "wrap") +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test",
                     label = "p.format", p.format = list(accuracy = 0.001, scale = 1),
                     tip.length = 0.02, step.increase = 0.1, size = 3.5, bracket.size = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = its_colors, labels = its_labels) +
  scale_color_manual(values = its_colors, labels = its_labels) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = its_labels) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(title = "plasma IL-8", x = "", y = "IL-8 (pg/mL, log10)") +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"))

# PANEL B: Angiopoietin-2
fig_b <- facts_its_rel_analysis %>%
  filter(!is.na(ang2), !is.na(Candida_Dominance_3cat)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Candida_Dominance_3cat, y = ang2,
             fill = Candida_Dominance_3cat, color = Candida_Dominance_3cat)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.4, alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA, size = 0.5) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.5, size = 2, cex = 2, corral = "wrap") +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test",
                     label = "p.format", p.format = list(accuracy = 0.001, scale = 1),
                     tip.length = 0.02, step.increase = 0.1, size = 3.5, bracket.size = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = its_colors, labels = its_labels) +
  scale_color_manual(values = its_colors, labels = its_labels) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = its_labels) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(title = "plasma Angiopoietin-2", x = "", y = "Ang-2 (pg/mL, log10)") +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"))

# PANEL C: TNFR1
fig_c <- facts_its_rel_analysis %>%
  filter(!is.na(tnfr1_final), !is.na(Candida_Dominance_3cat)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Candida_Dominance_3cat, y = tnfr1_final,
             fill = Candida_Dominance_3cat, color = Candida_Dominance_3cat)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.4, alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA, size = 0.5) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.5, size = 2, cex = 2, corral = "wrap") +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test",
                     label = "p.format", p.format = list(accuracy = 0.001, scale = 1),
                     tip.length = 0.02, step.increase = 0.1, size = 3.5, bracket.size = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = its_colors, labels = its_labels) +
  scale_color_manual(values = its_colors, labels = its_labels) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = its_labels) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(title = "plasma sTNFR1", x = "", y = "sTNFR1 (pg/mL, log10)") +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"))

# Combine into single row
fig_its_inflammatory <- fig_a + fig_b + fig_c +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = list(c('A', 'B', 'C')),
                  title = "Inflammatory Biomarkers by C. albicans Dominance",
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")))

print(fig_its_inflammatory)

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "figures", "Figure_ITS_Inflammatory_Biomarkers_Combined.pdf"),
       plot = fig_its_inflammatory, width = 12, height = 4.5, dpi = 300)


```


```{r clusters_updated_publication}

library(tidyverse)
library(ggpubr)
library(ggbeeswarm)
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(patchwork)

# Load and prepare cluster data
Mbiome_SampleMetadata_2.24.23 <- read_csv("Mbiome_SampleMetadata_2.24.23.csv")
MultiCompMbio_all_clusters_allCohorts_2 <- read_csv("MultiCompMbio_all_clusters_allCohorts_2.csv")

clustersMbio <- merge(Mbiome_SampleMetadata_2.24.23, MultiCompMbio_all_clusters_allCohorts_2, 
                      by = "sample_id", all = TRUE)

clustersMbio_lung_its <- clustersMbio %>%
  filter(SampleType.x == "ETA") %>%
  filter(SubjectID %in% facts_its_rel_analysis$SubjectID) %>%
  filter(Day == 1) %>%
  group_by(SubjectID) %>%
  slice_max(total16sreads, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

# Merge with facts_its
facts_its_clusters <- merge(facts_its_rel_analysis, clustersMbio_lung_its, by = "SubjectID", all.x = TRUE)

# Define comparisons
my_comparisons <- list(c("1", "2"), c("1", "3"))

# Color palette for clusters
cluster_colors <- c("1" = "brown3", "2" = "pink2", "3" = "steelblue")

# ===== PANEL A: Survival Curve =====
survival_fit <- survfit(Surv(survival_time90_fromICUAdmission, survival_status90) ~ 
                        lung_bacterial_fungal_cluster, 
                        data = facts_its_clusters)

fig_surv <- ggsurvplot(survival_fit, 
                       pval = TRUE,
                       pval.method = TRUE,
                       title = "90-Day Survival by Clusters",
                       legend.labs = c("Cluster 1", "Cluster 2", "Cluster 3"),
                       palette = c("brown3", "pink2", "steelblue"),
                       risk.table = TRUE,
                       tables.theme = clean_theme(),
                       legend.title = "Clusters",
                       font.legend = c(10, "bold", "black"),
                       font.title = c(12, "bold", "black"),
                       xlab = "Days Post ICU Admission",
                       ylab = "Probability of Survival",   
                       xlim = c(0, 90),  # ← LIMIT X-AXIS TO 90 DAYS
                       break.time.by = 15,  # ← NICE SPACING
                       size = 1.2)

# ===== PANEL B: C. albicans Abundance =====
fig_calb <- facts_its_clusters %>%
  filter(!is.na(lung_bacterial_fungal_cluster)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = as.factor(lung_bacterial_fungal_cluster), y = Calbicans_pct,
             fill = as.factor(lung_bacterial_fungal_cluster),
             color = as.factor(lung_bacterial_fungal_cluster))) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.4, alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA, size = 0.5) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.5, size = 2, cex = 2, corral = "wrap") +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test",
                     label = "p.format", p.format = list(accuracy = 0.001, scale = 1),
                     tip.length = 0.02, step.increase = 0.1, size = 3.5, bracket.size = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_colors) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cluster_colors) +
  labs(title = "C. albicans Abundance",
       x = "Bacterial-Fungal Cluster", y = "C. albicans Relative Abundance (%)") +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))

# ===== PANEL C: Bacterial Pathogen Abundance =====
fig_pathogen <- facts_its_clusters %>%
  filter(!is.na(lung_bacterial_fungal_cluster)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = as.factor(lung_bacterial_fungal_cluster), y = PathogenSum,
             fill = as.factor(lung_bacterial_fungal_cluster),
             color = as.factor(lung_bacterial_fungal_cluster))) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.4, alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA, size = 0.5) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.5, size = 2, cex = 2, corral = "wrap") +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test",
                     label = "p.format", p.format = list(accuracy = 0.001, scale = 1),
                     tip.length = 0.02, step.increase = 0.1, size = 3.5, bracket.size = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_colors) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cluster_colors) +
  labs(title = "Bacterial Pathogen Abundance",
       x = "Bacterial-Fungal Cluster", y = "Pathogen Relative Abundance (%)") +
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))

# ===== COMBINE: Abundance panels (top) + Survival (bottom) =====
abundance_combined <- fig_calb + fig_pathogen +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = list(c('A', 'B')),
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold")))

# Survival with risk table
surv_with_risk <- plot_grid(
  fig_surv$plot,
  fig_surv$table,
  ncol = 1,
  align = "v",
  axis = "lr",
  rel_heights = c(3, 1)
)

# Final combined figure
fig_clusters_final <- (abundance_combined / surv_with_risk) +
  plot_annotation(title = "Integrated Bacterial-Fungal Clusters and Clinical Outcomes",
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")))

print(fig_clusters_final)

# Save
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "figures", "Figure_Clusters_Combined.pdf"),
       plot = fig_clusters_final, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)

```

```{r clusters and cultures}
# ===== LOAD AND PREPARE DATA =====
clustersMbio_lung_cxpos <- clustersMbio %>%
  filter(SampleType.x == "ETA") %>%
  filter(SubjectID %in% factsalir_cxpos$SubjectID) %>%
  filter(Day == 1) %>%
  group_by(SubjectID) %>%
  slice_max(total16sreads, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

clustersMbio_lung_cxpos <- merge(clustersMbio_lung_cxpos, factsalir_cxpos, 
                                 by = "SubjectID", all.x = TRUE)


# ===== CONTINGENCY TABLE =====
culture_cluster_table <- table(clustersMbio_lung_cxpos$cxres_lrt_f, 
                              clustersMbio_lung_cxpos$lung_bacterial_fungal_cluster)

cat("\n════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("CULTURE RESULTS vs. BACTERIAL-FUNGAL CLUSTERS\n")
cat("════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")
print(culture_cluster_table)

# Calculate percentages
culture_cluster_pct <- prop.table(culture_cluster_table, margin = 1) * 100

cat("\n\nPERCENTAGES WITHIN EACH CULTURE CATEGORY:\n\n")
print(round(culture_cluster_pct, 1))

# ===== PREPARE DATA FOR VISUALIZATION =====
culture_cluster_data <- as.data.frame(culture_cluster_table) %>%
  rename(Culture = Var1, Cluster = Var2, Count = Freq) %>%
  group_by(Culture) %>%
  mutate(Pct = 100 * Count / sum(Count),
         Total = sum(Count)) %>%
  ungroup()

# ===== STACKED BAR CHART =====
fig_culture_cluster <- culture_cluster_data %>%
  mutate(Culture = factor(Culture, levels = c("NRF", "BacterialPathogen", "Candida")),
         Cluster = factor(Cluster, levels = c("1", "2", "3"))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Culture, y = Count, fill = Cluster)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.8, color = "black", size = 0.5) +
  
  # Add percentage labels
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.0f%%\n(n=%d)", Pct, Count)),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
            fontface = "bold", size = 3.5, color = "white") +
  
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("1" = "brown3", "2" = "pink2", "3" = "steelblue")
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Clinical Culture Categories and Integrative Bacterial-Fungal Clusters",
        x = "Clinical Culture Result",
    y = "Number of Subjects",
    fill = "16S-ITS Clusters"
  ) +
  
  theme_pubr() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, face = "italic"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

print(fig_culture_cluster)

# ===== SAVE =====
ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, "figures", "FigureSUPP_Culture_vs_Clusters_Overlap.pdf"),
  plot = fig_culture_cluster,
  width = 8,
  height = 5.5,
  dpi = 300
)


```
